pattern: Filter to points sharing a row or column with you, then take the one of minimum Manhattan distance, breaking ties by smallest index.
hints: Only points that share your x or your y can ever be valid candidates.
For a valid point sharing one coordinate, the Manhattan distance reduces to the difference in the other coordinate.
Scan once, tracking the best distance and its index; on a tie keep the earlier index, and return -1 if nothing qualifies.

edge_inputs.description: several valid points, ties broken by index
single valid point at min coords
later point is closer but tie picks earliest equal distance
mix of valid and invalid, nearest valid wins

stress.description: many random points, planted valid ones
